  "clarification_question": "Which focus should I apply: (A) sentence-level writing polish; (B) check the internal logic and claim–evidence alignment of the paragraph; or (C) high-level reframing/positioning of the contribution?",

    "R1.2": "The paragraph makes causal and interpretive claims (CTR improvement implying reduced interruption cost) that warrant a check for internal logical coherence.",

parsed.clarification_question: Which focus should I apply: (A) sentence-level writing polish; (B) check the internal logic and claim–evidence alignment of the paragraph; or (C) high-level reframing/positioning of the contribution?

rationale.R1.2: The paragraph makes causal and interpretive claims (CTR improvement implying reduced interruption cost) that warrant a check for internal logical coherence.
rationale.R1.3: Reported CTR numbers and study setup should be examined to see if they support the stated interpretations and contributions.
rationale.R1.6: The request to 'take a look' commonly entails sentence-level clarity, concision, and academic tone adjustments within the paragraph.
rationale.RX.1: Given limited context from a single paragraph, uncertainty about causal interpretation and generality should be explicitly noted.
rationale.RX.2: Care is needed to avoid endorsing overbroad or causal claims not strictly supported by the reported metrics.
rationale.RX.3: Scope will be limited strictly to this paragraph without inferring or suggesting changes to unseen sections or experiments.
rationale.RX.4: Any feedback will avoid encouraging practices that might unduly risk user privacy or well-being given the sensitive mobile context.
rationale.RX.5: Comments will tie back to specific sentences and figures stated in the provided text to maintain traceability.
